programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Jackie and Becky pitch their business plan to Darlene in hopes of getting the Lunch Box back, but Darlene's decision leads to hostility; Dan bans Odessa from the house, causing a rift with Harris; Mark makes extensive Thanksgiving plans.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Tim (Tim Allen) and the "Tool Time" staff take the show on the road when they ship off on an aircraft carrier.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Tim's obsession with daily routine prompts him and Jill to dream about their lives 35 years in the future.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: When Tim's boss tells the tale of his impending divorce, Tim decides to attend a couples workshop with Jill.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Randy (Jonathan Taylor Thomas) prepares to express his true feelings for Lauren, only to discover she loves another. With Tim Allen.

programme.title: Bob Hearts Abishola
programme.sub-title: A Tight Ass Is a Wonderful Thing
programme.desc: Thanks to Abishola's brutal honesty, Bob finds himself in a feud with Kemi; after Douglas makes a big mistake on the warehouse floor, Goodwin hates himself for looking the other way.

programme.title: Bob Hearts Abishola
programme.sub-title: Take Two Yellows and Go to Bed
programme.desc: Bob's grand opening party for the sock factory is upstaged by a fight between Abishola and her sister-in-law.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: Cheryl urges Jim to meet an old girlfriend (Linda Hamilton) for lunch, but has second thoughts when she becomes convinced the woman has romantic intentions.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: When Cheryl and Dana's mother, Maggie, visits for the holidays, Jim and Cheryl compete with Ryan and Dana for her affection.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Mike finds out that Ed is skipping Outdoor Man's annual bass festival in favor of Bonnie's fancy art gala; Mandy takes over festival planning for Kristin, and things don't go as planned.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Mike and Chuck compete in Outdoorsman's annual chili cook-off; Ryan and Kyle take the place of Kristin and Mandy at Vanessa's weekly mother-daughter luncheon.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Mike and Mandy go to extreme measures to help Vanessa connect with voters in her state assembly campaign; Ed's offer to split a baby gift for Kristin and Ryan leads Chuck and Kyle to wonder which of them makes more money.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: When Mike and Vanessa invite Jen's father to her graduation, Jen struggles with accepting his "plus one" -- his girlfriend Fiona; the Outdoor Man crew debates over which word was said in a viral audio recording that's been tearing the internet apart.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.sub-title: Diamonds Are a Ghoul's Best Friend
programme.desc: Jim is haunted by a ghost when he buys Cheryl a diamond necklace that was taken off a dead woman.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: In the last ever episode, Jim chokes on some food and finds himself on trial in heaven, where Andy represents the defense and Dana the Devil.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: When Jim tries to solve Ruby's separation anxiety, he discovers that he doesn't have the willpower to leave his teary 6-year-old at school.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: Cheryl convinces Jim to abstain from sex until their big getaway; Jim and Andy argue about a treehouse.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: When Tim (Tim Allen) tries to fix a gas leak in a friend's home, he accidentally blows up the building.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Tim (Tim Allen) and his sister-in-law (Jensen Daggett) accidentally see each other naked.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Tim and two bachelors from his audience discuss wives and girlfriends, while their mates tune in at home.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Instead of attending the theater with Jill and Ilene (Patricia Richardson, Sherry Hursey), Tim and Al (Tim Allen, Richard Karn) go out scalping hockey tickets.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Mike learns Ed hired his daughter to replace him as Outdoor Man's marketing consultant; Mike and Eve bring home a stray dog.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Vanessa blames Mike when she finds out they were not invited to a neighbor's party; Eve accidentally damages Mandy's car.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: When a history of Mike and Vanessa's house reveals a previous occupant died there, the family starts blaming things on a ghost; Ed keeps borrowing money from Kyle because he doesn't carry cash.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Mandy gets to meet Kim Kardashian; when the city orders Mike to cut down a tree on his property, he decides to organize a protest.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: Jackie fixes up Donna with Kelso's older brother (guest star Luke Wilson); Red and Bob clash over a Corvette.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: The gang disapproves of the way Casey treats Donna; Kelso gets a job in order to buy Jackie a St. Valentine's Day gift.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: Leo admits to Red that he fell in love with Kitty while she nursed him back to health after his car accident.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: Jealous of Kelso's modeling career, Jackie seeks solace in the arms of her manager at the cheese shop.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: Red helps the guys get revenge on Kelso for an April Fools' Day prank involving superglue; Donna misses her mother.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: After Red and Kitty go out of town, a cheerleader agrees to date Eric as long as he picks her up in the Corvette.

programme.title: Austin City Limits
programme.desc: A career-spanning hour with The Avett Brothers features new songs from their album "The Avett Brothers."

programme.title: NOVA
programme.desc: Archaeological investigations into the origins of democracy in Greece 2,500 years ago; mass graves and ancient ruins where tyrants were murdered shine light on the birth of a revolutionary form of government.

programme.title: NOVA
programme.desc: The military technologies that helped propel the colonies to victory, from the Brown Bess musket to the world's first military submarine.

programme.title: The Kimberley: Australia's Wild West
programme.desc: Exploring ancient fire traditions and a sugar glider's daring escape from the flames.

programme.title: Declarations: Black Americans and the Revolutionary War
programme.desc: Following the stories of enslaved and freed Black Americans who fought to define democracy and their liberty through the Revolutionary War.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A Thomas Hart Benton oil on tin; Kentucky sugar chest; a 1960 inscribed "To Kill a Mockingbird."

programme.title: Mister Rogers' Neighborhood
programme.desc: Bob Trow's dog Barney stays with Fred Rogers; King Friday wants to give Ana a parrot.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.sub-title: Arthur Makes Waves; It Came From Beyond
programme.desc: Arthur and D.W. look for a place to keep cool; Grandma Thora takes in a stray dog.

programme.title: Wild Kratts
programme.desc: The Kratt brothers take a day off from adventuring to go camping, where they meet a mischievous little moose calf.

programme.title: Weather Hunters
programme.desc: As a hurricane approaches, the Hunter kids learn about preparation and safety, moving furniture inside and checking their emergency supplies.

programme.title: Lyla in the Loop
programme.desc: Lyla and Everett coach Luke, Stu and Idris in soccer drills before their first practice; Liana and Louise decide to switch rooms with Lyla and Luke.
